hypothesis: A cross-domain universal model trained with a shared latent projection and a joint objective combining cross-modal energy-geometry consistency and cross-modal contrastive alignment across molecules, materials, and proteins will produce a universal representation of matter whose intrinsic dimensionality and manifold topology are dataset-invariant, and whose embeddings demonstrate higher cross-domain alignment (as measured by CKNNA and IDCor) and lower information imbalance on both in-distribution and out-of-distribution data than models trained with modality-specific objectives.
